page: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 64 dedicated physical cores using the AWS Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture running at 2.6 GHz. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache per core, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high memory efficiency. The instance achieved top-tier performance in Geekbench multi-core testing and software compilation using Clang, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Passmark memory scores also ranked in the top 10%, supported by strong uncached memory bandwidth write speeds of 116.96 GB/sec and read speeds of 253.34 GB/sec. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and database operations via Passmark both registered in the top 25%. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrated strong performance in Redis SET operations at 101.19 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 72.94 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and text generation for small LLM models scored in the average and weak categories, respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64-based Graviton architecture provides high memory density and strong multi-threaded performance without the overhead of hyperthreading, as each of the 64 vCPUs maps to a dedicated physical core. The 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth supports high-throughput data transfers. This profile makes the r7g.16xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the arm64 AWS Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance and multi-core execution, with Passmark memory scores and Geekbench software compilation results ranking in the top 10%. The instance also delivers strong performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving, backed by a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While single-threaded tasks and small-model text generation show average to weak performance, the dedicated core architecture and high memory capacity make this instance highly efficient for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 512 GB of RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized arm64 instance with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 512 GB of RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and half a terabyte of RAM for high-throughput database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated arm64 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz on AWS Neoverse-V1 architecture.
512.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core resource density.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core software compilation performance.
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer.
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching platforms, and multi-threaded workloads.
